title: Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 in Ghana (Full & Partial Funding Available)
summary: Apply for the 2026 Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future in Accra, Ghana The Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 is now open for applications. This fully immersive training program will take place from 18 August to 28 August 2026 in Accra, Ghana , bringing together professionals and changemakers across Anglophone Africa to strengthen governance in the extractive and energy sectors during a critical era of global energy transition. Hosted by ACEP with support from NRGI under the Anglophone Africa Extractive Industries Knowledge Hub (AFREIKH) , this one-week intensive summer school is designed to close knowledge gaps, build leadership capacity, and equip participants with practical tools for managing Africa’s natural resources more effectively. A limited number of full and partial scholarships are available for selected applicants. Why This Opportunity Matters Africa’s extractive industries remain central to economic growth, public revenue generation, and foreign exchange earnings. However, many countries continue to face governance challenges such as: Weak institutional systems Revenue leakages Limited local value addition Unequal distribution of resource benefits Poor community outcomes Environmental and social concerns As global markets shift toward cleaner energy systems, Africa must develop new governance models that ensure a just, inclusive, and sustainable energy transition . This summer school provides the knowledge, networks, and policy insights needed to help shape that future. Program Details Program Name: Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 Dates: 18 August – 28 August 2026 Location: Accra, Ghana Format: In-person Time: Starts 9:00 AM | Ends 9:00 AM WAT Funding: Full Scholarships, Partial Scholarships, Self-Funded Options Available What Participants Will Learn Participants will engage in high-level discussions, practical sessions, and policy analysis on the evolving dynamics of Africa’s extractive industries and energy systems. Core Topics Include: Energy transition under fiscal and governance constraints Maximizing economic benefits in energy transitions Environmental and Social Governance (ESG) in extractives Community outcomes and benefit sharing Just transition frameworks Geopolitics and strategies for resource mobilization Sustainable governance of natural resources Exclusive Conference Access Participants will also attend the Future of Energy Conference (FEC) , where they will explore innovative solutions, strategic partnerships, and forward-thinking approaches for Africa’s sustainable energy future. Benefits of the Summer School Successful applicants will receive access to: World-class training from sector experts Exposure to cutting-edge policy discussions Networking with professionals across Africa Practical governance and leadership skills Participation in the Future of Energy Conference Mentorship and professional development opportunities Membership in a vibrant alumni network Full or partial scholarship opportunities Eligibility Criteria To apply, applicants must: Be a citizen of an Anglophone African country Hold a role in an institution working in extractives, energy, governance, media, academia, parliament, or related sectors Have at least three years of relevant professional experience Demonstrate strong interest in natural resource governance and energy transition issues Be available to participate fully in the entire program Have strong written and spoken English skills Required Documents Applicants must prepare: Completed application form Curriculum Vitae (CV) Program Fee The standard participation fee is USD 2,500 , which covers: Tuition Accommodation Meals Local transportation Program logistics Note: International flights and visa costs are not included. Scholarship Options A limited number of scholarships will be awarded competitively after the review process. Applicants can choose one of the following funding options: Fully Funded (Scholarship Required) Partially Funded (Covers tuition, meals, and local transportation) Self-Funded / Organization-Funded Application Deadline Deadline: 8 May 2026 Early application is strongly recommended due to limited scholarship slots. How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the official application process through the organizers’ platform. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more summer school opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 is an outstanding opportunity for African professionals who want to drive better governance, sustainable development, and inclusive growth in the continent’s energy and extractive sectors. If you are passionate about shaping Africa’s future in the era of energy transition, this program is for you. title: Visa Africa Accelerator Program 2026: Unlocking Funding, Mentorship, and $200,000+ in Startup Growth Perks for African Fintech Founders
summary: The African fintech ecosystem continues to experience rapid transformation, driven by innovation in payments, embedded finance, digital banking, and SME-focused financial solutions. However, despite this growth, many startups still face major challenges such as limited access to funding, difficulty scaling across markets, and lack of structured mentorship. To address these gaps, Visa has launched the Visa Africa Accelerator Program 2026 (Cohort 6) , a high-impact initiative designed to support early-stage fintech startups across Africa. This program offers founders access to investment opportunities, expert mentorship, technical tools, and over $200,000 in product perks and resources to accelerate business growth. Applications are now officially open. APPLY HERE Application Deadline: May 17, 2026 Overview of the Visa Africa Accelerator Program 2026 The Visa Africa Accelerator Program is a structured startup development initiative focused on helping fintech founders refine, scale, and expand their solutions across African markets and beyond. It is designed for startups operating in or expanding into Africa that are building innovative financial solutions in areas such as: Digital payments and payment infrastructure Embedded finance solutions SME financial empowerment tools Financial inclusion technologies Digital banking and lending platforms Cross-border payment systems Through this program, startups gain access to Visa’s global ecosystem, investor networks, and technical expertise, helping them move from early traction to scalable market leadership. Why This Accelerator Matters for African Startups Africa’s fintech landscape is one of the fastest-growing in the world. However, many founders struggle with: Scaling beyond local markets Accessing venture capital funding Building compliant financial infrastructure Refining product-market fit Navigating regulatory environments The Visa Africa Accelerator addresses these challenges by offering a complete startup growth ecosystem that combines funding access, mentorship, and technical enablement. This program is especially valuable for founders seeking to: Expand into regional and global markets Improve product scalability and infrastructure Attract institutional and venture investors Build stronger, investor-ready business models Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Visa Africa Accelerator 2026, startups must meet the following requirements: 1. Stage of Startup Seed stage to Series A startups Must have a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) or a market-ready product 2. Product Focus Startups must be working in fintech-related innovation such as: Payments and digital transactions Embedded finance systems Financial infrastructure development SME financial solutions Inclusive financial services 3. Traction Requirement Demonstrated market traction Evidence of user adoption or active customer base 4. Geographic Requirement Startups must be based in Africa OR Have a clear expansion strategy targeting African markets Key Benefits of the Program Selected startups will receive extensive support across multiple growth dimensions: 1. Expert Training Capacity Building Participants gain structured learning in: Product development and design thinking Financial management and startup finance Sales strategy and customer acquisition Marketing and growth scaling strategies 2. One-on-One Mentorship Direct access to experienced founders and fintech leaders Personalized business guidance Strategic growth planning support 3. Technical Enablement Access to Visa developer tools and fintech infrastructure Technical training to improve product scalability Guidance on building secure and compliant solutions 4. Investor Access and Demo Day Opportunity to pitch to global investors Exposure to venture capital firms and strategic partners Participation in Demo Day with funding visibility 5. $200,000+ in Product Perks Startup credits and discounts from over 100 global partners Tools and resources for product development, operations, and scaling 6. Strategic Partnerships Networking with global fintech leaders Access to industry partnerships that support expansion How to Apply Interested startups can apply through the official Visa Africa Accelerator portal: https://africa.visa.com/en_MW/visa-everywhere/innovation/visa-accelerator.html Applicants are encouraged to prepare: A clear product description Evidence of traction Business model overview Growth and expansion strategy Deadline Reminder All applications must be submitted on or before: May 17, 2026 Late applications will not be considered. Conclusion The Visa Africa Accelerator Program 2026 represents one of the most strategic opportunities for African fintech founders aiming to scale their innovations globally. With strong backing from Visa, access to investors, technical tools, and over $200,000 in startup support resources, this program is designed to transform early-stage startups into scalable, investment-ready companies. For ambitious founders in Africa’s fintech space, this is a critical opportunity to accelerate growth and gain global visibility. For More information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more accelerator programs here. title: 2027-28 Fulbright Postgraduate Scholarships to the UK for US Citizens | Fully Funded Opportunity
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious 2027-28 US-UK Fulbright Commission Postgraduate Awards to the United Kingdom . This highly competitive international scholarship gives US citizens the opportunity to pursue postgraduate study or independent research at recognized universities across the UK. As part of the renowned Fulbright US Student Program, these awards provide one of the most valuable pathways for American students and emerging professionals to gain world-class education, conduct impactful research, and build lifelong global networks. If you have dreams of studying in England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland while expanding your academic and professional horizons, this is an opportunity worth considering. Why Apply for the Fulbright Postgraduate Awards? The Fulbright Program is globally recognized for fostering leadership, academic excellence, and cross-cultural understanding. Scholars are selected not only for academic merit but also for their ability to serve as ambassadors of cultural exchange. Studying in the UK through Fulbright allows you to: Access top-ranked UK universities Develop international research experience Build leadership and intercultural skills Expand career opportunities worldwide Join a global alumni network of accomplished professionals Experience British culture through exclusive enrichment programs Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants generally receive: Full or partial tuition waiver Personal living allowance (stipend) Sickness and accident coverage Cultural enrichment programmes and events Membership to national arts and culture organisations Access to Fulbright’s global alumni network of over 300,000 members The living allowance is designed to help cover student living costs in the UK and may be reviewed annually. Eligible Fields of Study The Fulbright US-UK Commission offers multiple awards across a wide range of academic disciplines in partnership with universities throughout the UK. Applicants may also apply for an Open Award , which supports postgraduate study or independent research projects at any recognized UK university. Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must: Be a US citizen Be a non-UK resident Hold a bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience before departure Demonstrate academic excellence and leadership potential Show intercultural sensitivity and ambassadorial qualities Have a genuine interest in learning about the UK and sharing American culture Demonstrate community involvement and extracurricular engagement Dual US-UK citizens are also eligible. Ineligible Programs The following are not eligible under this scheme: Part-time postgraduate degrees Distance learning programs Part-time research courses What Selection Committees Look For Competitive applicants typically demonstrate: Strong academic background Leadership potential Community service involvement Clear reasons for selecting a UK university Plans to contribute to the Fulbright mission after returning to the US Ability to promote mutual understanding between the US and UK Application Process Applications are submitted through the Institute of International Education (IIE) under the Fulbright US Student Program. Applicants must also submit separate applications to their chosen UK universities, following each institution’s deadlines and admission requirements. Key Timeline Applications Opened: 31 March 2026 Application Deadline: 6 October 2026, 5 PM EST Interviews: February – March 2027 Final Notification: April 2027 Onboarding: May – September 2027 Arrival in UK: September 2027 How to Apply Interested applicants should review available awards, choose a suitable UK university, and complete their Fulbright application through the official program portal. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts The Fulbright Postgraduate Awards to the UK offer far more than financial support. They provide a transformational international experience that can shape your academic path, career growth, and global perspective. For eligible US citizens with ambitions to study or research in the United Kingdom, this is one of the best fully funded opportunities available. title: WHO JPO Technical Officer Job in Denmark 2026: Fully Paid Global Health Opportunity for Young Professionals
summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) Regional Office for Europe is now accepting applications for the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) – Technical Officer (Sexual, Reproductive, Maternal, Newborn, Child Adolescent Health) position based in Copenhagen, Denmark . This is a prestigious early-career international opportunity for talented professionals who want to build a career in global health while contributing to life-changing work across the WHO European Region. Selected candidates will support policies, research, technical programs, and country-level implementation focused on maternal health, adolescent health, sexual and reproductive rights, and stronger health systems. If you are passionate about improving healthcare outcomes and working with one of the world’s leading health organizations, this opportunity could be the next step in your career. Application Deadline Apply Before: 05 May 2026, 04:59 PM Opportunity Overview Organization: World Health Organization (WHO) Programme: Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Position Title: Technical Officer Location: Copenhagen, Denmark Job Type: Full-time Grade: P2 Contract Duration: 1 year (with possibility of extension) Practice Area: Health About the Role The selected Junior Professional Officer will work under WHO’s Division of Health Systems and contribute to advancing Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR) across the life course. The role includes a strong focus on: Maternal and newborn health outcomes Adolescent sexual and reproductive health Quality of care in health facilities Evidence-based health policies Monitoring and evaluation systems Country support and implementation strategies Partnerships with UN agencies and international stakeholders This position offers hands-on exposure to global policy development, health systems strengthening, and multilateral cooperation. Key Responsibilities Successful candidates may support WHO in the following areas: Assess maternal, newborn, and adolescent health situations across member states Review implementation of health interventions and service quality Support countries in adopting WHO-recommended strategies Develop indicators and standards for reproductive and maternal care Monitor progress on sexual and reproductive rights outcomes Analyze country data and prepare technical reports Organize regional meetings and knowledge-sharing events Engage with national focal points and development partners Benefits of the WHO JPO Programme Joining the WHO JPO Programme comes with significant career and professional advantages: 1. International Career Growth Gain direct experience working with WHO and global partners in an international environment. 2. Real Global Impact Contribute to programs that improve health outcomes for women, newborns, adolescents, and vulnerable populations. 3. Professional Development Build leadership, technical, and policy skills through structured supervision and training. 4. Financial Support for Learning The role includes a $4,000 annual Duty Travel and Training Allowance (DTTA) for career development and learning activities. 5. Networking Opportunities Collaborate with WHO experts, governments, UN agencies, and global health professionals. Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates should typically meet the following requirements: Education Doctor of Medicine, Master’s degree in Public Health, or related field In some cases, Bachelor’s degree holders with additional experience may qualify Experience Minimum 2 years relevant experience after first degree 4 years for Bachelor’s degree applicants Language Excellent written and spoken English Age Maximum 32 years old by 31 December of the application year Additional Criteria Limited UN experience (maximum 1 year) National of an eligible country Academic studies partly completed in an eligible developing country Required Documents Applicants are expected to prepare: Motivation letter in English Passport copy Educational certificates/degrees Why You Should Apply The WHO JPO Programme is one of the best pathways for young professionals seeking a long-term career in international development and public health. It combines meaningful work, professional mentorship, and global exposure in one of the most respected organizations in the world. If you want to create impact through evidence-based health solutions and international cooperation, this is a valuable opportunity to pursue. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official WHO/UN recruitment portal before the deadline. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . title: NASA Force Jobs 2026: Apply for NASA’s New Engineering & Technology Hiring Program Before April 21
summary: NASA has launched an exciting new hiring initiative called NASA Force , designed to recruit the next generation of engineers, technologists, and innovators for mission-critical roles in space exploration, aeronautics, and scientific discovery. This new opportunity gives early- to mid-career professionals the chance to contribute directly to some of the most important projects shaping the future of humanity. If you have technical skills, a passion for innovation, and the drive to solve complex challenges, NASA Force could be your pathway to working with one of the most respected organizations in the world. What is NASA Force? NASA Force is a specialized hiring program developed in partnership with the U.S. Office of Personnel Management. It aims to bring highly skilled technical professionals into focused term appointments, usually lasting 1–2 years , with the possibility of extension. Participants will join collaborative teams working on advanced technologies, human spaceflight systems, scientific missions, and next-generation aerospace solutions. This initiative is part of NASA’s strategy to strengthen America’s leadership in air and space while accelerating innovation across multiple mission areas. Why You Should Apply for NASA Force Joining NASA Force offers far more than a traditional job. Selected candidates will gain the opportunity to: Work on high-impact NASA projects Contribute to real missions in space, aeronautics, and science Collaborate with top engineers, scientists, and innovators Build expertise through hands-on technical experience Receive mentorship and participate in knowledge-sharing initiatives Expand career opportunities in one of the world’s top institutions Solve meaningful problems with global impact Example Projects You May Work On NASA Force participants may support groundbreaking initiatives such as: VIPER lunar rover operations Deep space logistics systems NASA Spaceport 2.0 development Orion flight software and real-time operating systems Lunar and astromaterials sample curation In-situ resource utilization (ISRU) systems for lunar bases AI/ML solutions for air traffic control automation Propulsion systems for Artemis, Commercial Crew, and Launch Services These projects represent some of the most advanced engineering and scientific work in the world. Who Should Apply? NASA Force is ideal for: Engineers Software developers Data scientists Aerospace professionals AI/ML specialists Technical researchers Systems engineers Innovation leaders Mid-career STEM professionals seeking impact-driven roles If you want your work to matter and contribute to future exploration, this opportunity is worth serious consideration. Appointment Details Program Type: Term Employment Duration: Typically 1–2 years Extension Possible: Yes Work Focus: Mission-critical technical roles Organization: NASA How to Apply All applications must be submitted through the U.S. federal hiring portal, USAJOBS . Application Steps: Create or log into your USAJOBS account Build or upload your federal resume (maximum two pages) Search for NASA Force openings Submit your application before the closing date Complete any required USA Hire assessment after applying Important Deadline Applications opened on April 17 and close on April 21. Interested candidates should apply immediately before the deadline expires. Official Links NASA Careers Website USAJOBS Official Portal NASA How to Apply Page APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . Pro Tip for Applicants Tailor your resume specifically to the vacancy announcement. Use measurable achievements, technical tools, project outcomes, and keywords from the job description to improve your chances of being shortlisted. Final Thoughts NASA Force is one of the most exciting technical hiring opportunities available in 2026. It offers skilled professionals a rare chance to work on world-changing missions while growing their careers inside NASA. If you are passionate about engineering, exploration, and innovation, don’t miss this opportunity. Submit your application before April 21. title: USDA-FSIS Graduate Student Food Safety Fellowship 2026 in the USA | $1,800 Monthly Stipend + $6,000 Research Support
summary: The USDA-FSIS Graduate Student Food Safety Fellowship 2026 is now open for applications, offering doctoral students in the United States a unique opportunity to contribute to national food safety research while receiving generous financial support. This prestigious fellowship is administered through the Oak Ridge Institute for Science and Education (ORISE) in partnership with the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS). Designed for students pursuing advanced research careers, the fellowship allows participants to develop innovative solutions that strengthen food safety systems, improve public health, and support science-based decision-making across the U.S. food supply chain. Why This Fellowship Matters Food safety remains one of the most important public health priorities in the United States. Through this fellowship, selected doctoral students will work on research projects aligned with USDA-FSIS priorities, helping address critical challenges in meat, poultry, and egg product safety. Participants will gain real-world experience in government-backed research, build professional networks, and present findings directly to senior leaders and scientists at FSIS. Fellowship Benefits Successful applicants will receive: $1,800 monthly stipend $6,000 total funding for travel or research supplies One-year appointment (renewable based on funding and performance) Flexible start date beginning August 3, 2026 Mentorship from university and FSIS experts Opportunity to visit FSIS laboratories and regulated food establishments Exposure to national food safety policy and scientific decision-making Strong academic and professional networking opportunities Research Areas Applicants are encouraged to submit proposals aligned with USDA-FSIS food safety priorities, including: Chemical hazard detection and intervention strategies Pathogen detection and foodborne illness prevention Whole genome sequencing and antimicrobial resistance research Food contamination prevention Animal welfare studies Label verification methods Consumer food handling education Data analytics in food safety Laboratory and non-laboratory food safety studies Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must: Be currently pursuing a doctoral degree Study food science, food safety, public health, veterinary medicine, or related disciplines Be enrolled in an accredited U.S. institution Be a U.S. citizen Demonstrate research interest in food safety Have strong communication skills Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Completed online application Unofficial transcript(s) Current resume/CV 500-word essay describing proposed research project Letter of support from university major professor/mentor One additional educational or professional recommendation All documents must be submitted in English Important Dates Application Deadline: June 5, 2026, at 3:00 PM Eastern Time Expected Start Date: August 3, 2026 How to Apply Applicants should submit their application through the official ORISE opportunity portal. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Pro Tip for Applicants Focus your 500-word essay on a practical food safety challenge and clearly explain how your research can improve the safety of the U.S. food supply. Strong, impact-driven proposals are more competitive. Final Thoughts The USDA-FSIS Graduate Student Food Safety Fellowship 2026 is an outstanding opportunity for doctoral students seeking funding, mentorship, and national-level research exposure. If your passion lies in protecting public health through science, this fellowship could be a major step forward in your academic and professional journey. title: NASA FINESST 2025 Research Grants for Graduate Students in Earth and Space Science
summary: The NASA FINESST 2025 funding opportunity provides Master’s and PhD students in Earth and space science with direct support to lead their own independent research projects. As part of NASA’s Research Opportunities in Space and Earth Sciences (ROSES) program, FINESST is designed to empower the next generation of researchers to develop innovative scientific ideas that advance understanding of Earth systems, space environments, and planetary science. This opportunity is specifically targeted at graduate students, offering them the chance to secure funding, gain research experience, and contribute to NASA’s long-term scientific missions. About the Program FINESST (Future Investigators in NASA Earth and Space Science and Technology) is a competitive graduate student research program funded by NASA. It supports students pursuing Master’s and PhD degrees who are conducting research aligned with NASA’s Science Mission Directorate priorities. Unlike large institutional grants, FINESST is designed to directly fund students as principal investigators of their own research proposals. This makes it a unique opportunity in the academic landscape, allowing students to take ownership of scientific projects while receiving financial support and mentorship through their academic institutions. The program is part of NASA’s broader commitment to strengthening early-career scientific talent and ensuring continued innovation in Earth and space sciences. Why This Opportunity Matters Graduate research often depends on limited funding and institutional resources. FINESST addresses this gap by giving students the independence and financial backing to pursue original scientific questions without relying entirely on faculty-led funding structures. This program plays a key role in shaping future leaders in Earth observation, climate science, astrophysics, and planetary exploration. By supporting student-led research, NASA ensures that emerging scientists can contribute fresh perspectives to some of the most important scientific challenges of our time, including climate change, environmental monitoring, and space exploration. For students, FINESST is more than a grant — it is an entry point into NASA’s research ecosystem and a foundation for long-term academic and professional growth. What Participants Will Gain Selected applicants receive funding to support their research activities over multiple years, including data collection, analysis, fieldwork, and computational modeling. Beyond funding, participants gain the opportunity to develop independent research leadership skills, strengthen their academic profiles, and build connections within NASA’s scientific community. The program also enhances competitiveness for future fellowships, postdoctoral positions, and research careers. Students benefit from working on high-impact scientific questions while contributing to research that supports NASA’s missions and global scientific understanding. Who Can Apply FINESST is open to students enrolled in Master’s or PhD programs at accredited institutions. Applicants must be graduate students at the time of submission and must submit proposals through their academic institution. Each proposal must be student-led, with faculty advisors providing guidance but not serving as principal investigators. International students enrolled at eligible U.S. institutions may also be able to participate, depending on institutional eligibility. Eligible Areas Proposals must align with NASA’s Science Mission Directorate, which includes: Earth Science (climate systems, environment, natural hazards) Heliophysics (Sun–Earth interactions and space weather) Planetary Science (solar system formation and exploration) Astrophysics (universe, galaxies, and cosmic phenomena) Biological and Physical Sciences (effects of space environments on systems) Research can include theoretical work, data analysis, modeling, laboratory studies, or observational science. Application Process and Deadline Applications must be submitted electronically through NASA’s NSPIRES system. Students are required to prepare a detailed research proposal outlining objectives, methodology, and expected outcomes, along with a supporting budget and institutional documentation. The submission deadline for FINESST 2025 is July 14, 2026 . Office hours will be held by NASA Earth Science teams on June 23 and June 24 (1–2 PM ET) for applicants seeking clarification. Proposals undergo peer review based on scientific merit, feasibility, and relevance to NASA’s mission priorities. Successful applicants are typically notified several months after submission. Learn more and apply on the official NSPIRES website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post NASA FINESST 2025 Research Grants for Graduate Students in Earth and Space Science appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: South Africa–Canada Seed Grant for Collaborative Research 2026 | Funding for International Research Partnerships
summary: The South Africa–Canada Seed Grant for Collaborative Research offers researchers a unique opportunity to secure funding while building impactful international partnerships between South Africa and Canada. Designed for PhD-level researchers, this program supports collaborative projects that address global challenges through interdisciplinary research and capacity development. About the Program Launched through a partnership between the National Research Foundation (NRF) of South Africa and the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council (SSHRC) of Canada, this initiative strengthens academic collaboration under the South Africa–Canada Universities Network (SACUN). The program aims to foster long-term research relationships by supporting joint projects between researchers affiliated with eligible institutions in both countries. Up to eight projects will be funded, with each focusing on shared global priorities. Funded projects will run for 24 to 36 months , beginning in late 2026, and will support a wide range of collaborative activities such as joint publications, workshops, conferences, and research exchanges. Why This Opportunity Matters International collaboration is increasingly essential for addressing complex global challenges such as climate change, inequality, and technological transformation. This grant provides a structured pathway for researchers to build meaningful cross-border partnerships while contributing to impactful, real-world solutions. Beyond funding, the program strengthens academic networks, promotes knowledge exchange, and supports inclusive research ecosystems. It also emphasizes capacity building, particularly for emerging researchers, making it a strategic opportunity for long-term academic growth. What Participants Will Gain Participants in this program benefit from both financial support and professional development opportunities that extend beyond a single project. Researchers will gain access to: Funding to support collaborative research activities and mobility Opportunities to build and expand international research networks Experience working on interdisciplinary and globally relevant topics Increased visibility through joint publications and research outputs Capacity-building opportunities for early-career researchers and students For South African teams, funding can reach up to ZAR 300,000 per year for three years , while Canadian researchers may receive up to CAD $100,000 over two years , with the possibility of a one-year extension. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to researchers who meet specific academic and institutional requirements in both countries. Applicants must: Hold a PhD Be affiliated with an eligible South African or Canadian postsecondary institution Apply as part of a joint research team with one Principal Investigator (PI) from each country Researchers from NGOs, private companies, or other sectors may participate as collaborators but cannot serve as Principal Investigators or receive funding directly from the grant. Eligible Areas Projects must align with one of the program’s priority themes, which reflect shared global and national interests: Just and Resilient Societies Environmental Sustainability Artificial Intelligence and Digital Innovation Sustainable Economic Growth Mathematics Education Interdisciplinary and transdisciplinary approaches are strongly encouraged, particularly those that integrate social sciences and humanities perspectives. Application Process and Deadline Applications must be submitted through a joint process led by the South African Principal Investigator via the NRF online system. Required documents include: CVs of all project partners Detailed project description Comprehensive budget breakdown Letters of intent from all collaborators The application deadline is May 28, 2026 , with institutional submissions due shortly after. Selected projects are expected to begin in October 2026 . Given the competitive nature of the program and the limited number of funded projects, applicants are encouraged to develop strong, well-structured proposals that clearly demonstrate collaboration, impact, and feasibility. Read the official call and apply on the Carelton University website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Fully Funded University of Birmingham MSc Development Policy and Politics Scholarship 2026 (Mo Ibrahim Foundation Internship Included)
summary: The University of Birmingham , through its International Development Department (IDD), in partnership with the Mo Ibrahim Foundation , is offering a prestigious fully funded scholarship for the MSc in Development Policy and Politics. This unique two-year programme combines academic excellence with practical experience, providing scholars with a one-year master’s degree followed by a fully integrated one-year internship. This opportunity is specifically designed to empower talented African students who are passionate about governance, leadership, and development policy. The programme emphasizes the critical role of politics in development and equips scholars with the analytical and practical skills needed to address global and regional challenges. Overview of the MSc Development Policy and Politics Programme The MSc Development Policy and Politics programme is grounded in the understanding that “politics matters” in shaping development outcomes. The course explores how governance, power structures, and policy decisions influence development at national and global levels. Students benefit from: A multidisciplinary curriculum Exposure to real-world policy challenges A diverse and international student body Research-led teaching informed by cutting-edge academic work The programme bridges the gap between theory and practice, enabling students to apply academic insights to real-world development contexts. Programme Structure The scholarship covers a two-year pathway , structured as follows: Year 1: MSc Academic Programme Students complete a full-time master’s degree with the following components: Core Modules International Development (20 credits) Development Policy and Politics (20 credits) Dissertation 60-credit research project Opportunity for overseas fieldwork Optional Modules Students can choose from a wide range of modules, including: Governance for Development Democracy, Dictatorship and Development Additional modules across the School of Government Year 2: Internship Programme The second year is dedicated to a structured internship experience , divided into two phases: 1. International Development Department (IDD) 6 months of research work Collaboration with academics and GSDRC 2. Mo Ibrahim Foundation (London) 6 months working on governance initiatives Participation in: Now Generation Forum Ibrahim Forum Ibrahim Governance Weekend This internship provides hands-on exposure to high-level policy work and leadership initiatives focused on Africa’s development. Scholarship Benefits The scholarship is fully funded and includes: Full tuition fees Return airfare to and from the United Kingdom Visa costs Monthly stipend of £1,592 (for 18 months) Arrival allowance of £1,082 Additionally: The final 6 months of internship funding is provided directly by the Mo Ibrahim Foundation This comprehensive financial package ensures that scholars can focus entirely on their academic and professional development. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for this scholarship, applicants must meet strict academic and demographic requirements: Academic Requirements A First-Class Honours degree or equivalent Degree must be in a relevant field such as: International Relations Politics Sociology Development Studies Public Administration Anthropology Environmental Studies Education Human Geography Psychology Diplomacy (Note: Business-related degrees are excluded) Age Requirement Must be under 30 years old at the time of application Nationality Requirement Must be an African national Must be domiciled or permanently resident in an African country Preference is given to applicants from Sub-Saharan Africa English Language Requirement IELTS: Minimum 6.5 TOEFL: Minimum 580 / 93 Application Process The application process consists of two stages : Stage 1: Pre-Application Screening Submit a screening form as early as possible Stage 2: Full Application Eligible candidates will receive an invitation Submit full application before the deadline Application Deadline Friday, 24 April 2026 (17:00 UK time) Why This Scholarship Matters This programme is more than just a degree—it is a leadership pipeline for Africa’s future policymakers and change-makers . Scholars gain: Academic excellence from a globally recognized university Real-world experience with a leading African governance foundation Access to elite policy networks and forums Skills to influence governance and development outcomes Contact Information University of Birmingham Edgbaston, Birmingham B15 2TT, United Kingdom Telephone: +44 (0)121 414 3344 Click HERE to learn more and apply for the University of Birmingham MSc Development Policy and Politics Scholarship 2026 program. Visit OFY for more global Scholarship and Internship opportunities. title: Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Study Opportunity in South Korea for East African Students
summary: The Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026 is now open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for students from East Africa to pursue undergraduate and graduate studies in South Korea with substantial financial support. This scholarship is made possible through a partnership between Daejin University and Inter-University Council for East Africa , both of which are committed to advancing higher education, research, and international collaboration. With access to globally recognized degrees, modern facilities, and an international learning environment, this program is a gateway for ambitious students to build a strong academic and professional future. Overview of the Scholarship This scholarship program targets students from East African Community (EAC) member states , providing them with the opportunity to study in English-taught programs at Daejin University. The scholarship supports both: 🎓 Undergraduate Program (International Business – 4 years) 🎓 Master’s Program (Education – 2 years) The initiative aims to strengthen academic exchange between East Africa and South Korea while equipping students with globally competitive skills. Application Deadline Deadline: 30 April 2026 Applicants must ensure that all required documents are submitted before the deadline to be considered for the selection process. Scholarship Benefits Daejin University offers generous financial support to selected candidates: Undergraduate Students 100% tuition fee waiver (Year 1) 100% dormitory fee waiver (Year 1) Limited slots: 16 positions (2 per EAC country) Master’s Students 100% tuition fee waiver (Semester 1) 100% dormitory fee waiver (Semester 1) Unlimited slots Additional Opportunities Performance-based scholarships for subsequent semesters Access to part-time work opportunities in South Korea Scholarship Renewal Conditions Scholarship continuation depends on academic performance (GPA) : Undergraduate Renewal GPA 4.3+ → 100% tuition waiver GPA 3.0–4.2 → 40% waiver GPA 2.5–2.99 → 20% waiver Below 2.5 → No waiver Master’s Renewal GPA 3.5+ → 40% waiver Below 3.5 → 20% waiver This performance-based model encourages academic excellence throughout the program. What Students Must Cover While the scholarship is generous, recipients are responsible for: ✈️ Return airfare 📄 Application fees 📚 Books and academic supplies 💰 Personal and living expenses (including meals) 🏥 Health insurance Applicants must also provide proof of financial capacity to support these costs. Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must meet the following requirements: 🌍 Citizenship Applicants must be citizens of the following EAC countries: Burundi Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) Kenya Rwanda South Sudan Tanzania Uganda Somalia 🎓 Academic Requirements Completion of O’Level and A’Level (or equivalent) Graduation years: 2023, 2024, or 2025 Minimum grade of B in Mathematics or English 📊 Age Limits Undergraduate: Below 25 years Master’s: Below 30 years 🌐 English Proficiency IELTS 5.5 / CEFR B2 / TOEFL iBT 59 or higher 💰 Financial Proof Applicants must provide: 6-month bank statement Minimum balance of $16,000 Sponsor must be self, parent, or legal guardian Application Process The selection process is conducted in two main stages : Stage 1: IUCEA Screening Submit application via online form Send required documents to official email Initial evaluation and shortlisting Stage 2: Daejin University Selection Interview via Zoom Document verification Final selection Final Confirmation Successful candidates receive admission and visa support documentation. Required Documents Applicants must submit one single PDF file containing: Statement of Interest Updated CV/Resume Passport copy Academic transcripts and certificates English proficiency certificate Financial proof documents Daejin University application form Incomplete or incorrectly formatted applications may not be considered. Admission Timeline 30 April 2026: Application deadline 8 May 2026: Preliminary selection 15 May 2026: Submission to Daejin University 19 June 2026: Interviews and admission results June–July 2026: Visa processing 20–30 August 2026: Arrival in Korea 1 September 2026: Start of studies Campus Life at Daejin University Daejin University offers a vibrant campus environment with modern facilities, including: 💻 Computer labs 📚 Study and seminar rooms 💪 Gymnasium 🧺 Laundry and kitchen facilities 🏠 Comfortable dormitories Students also benefit from a supportive international community and opportunities to gain work experience through part-time jobs. Why Study in South Korea? South Korea is known for its advanced education system, technological innovation, and dynamic culture. Studying at Daejin University allows students to experience: High-quality education Global career opportunities Cultural exchange and networking Exposure to one of Asia’s leading economies Final Thoughts The Daejin University – IUCEA Scholarship 2026 is a highly competitive and rewarding opportunity for East African students seeking international education. With strong financial support, access to quality education, and opportunities for personal and professional growth, this scholarship can be a transformative step toward a global career. If you meet the eligibility criteria, start preparing your application early and take advantage of this opportunity to study in South Korea.
